MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-09-2021 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT KIM LAVALLE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 430.41. CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 430.41 REQUIRES A PARTY, BEFORE FILING THE DEMURRER, TO "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" WITH THE PARTY WHO FILED THE COMPLAINT AND TO FILE A DECLARATION STATING THE MEANS BY WHICH THE PARTIES MET AND CONFERRED. THE JANUARY 5, 2021 "DECLARATION RE: MEET AND CONFER OBLIGATION" IS INSUFFICIENT. DEFENDANT SHALL IMMEDIATELY MEET AND CONFER WITH THE PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS DUE NO LATER THAN MARCH 9, 2021. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2021 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT KIM LAVALLE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Kim Lavalle's perfunctory demurrer to plaintiff Vicki Marquez's complaint is overruled in its entirety. The complaint properly alleges negligence and premises liability causes of action. Plaintiff alleges she fell on a staircase at a property she rented from defendant. (Compl., pp. 3-4.) The property and staircase were owned, operated, maintained, repaired, and controlled by defendant. (Id. at p.4.) Defendant was negligent and careless in the maintenance and creation of the staircase which led to plaintiff's injury. (Id.) Further, plaintiff alleges defendant knew about the dangerous and illegal staircase. (Id. at p.5.) The complaint is not conclusory and properly alleges the elements of duty and breach for each cause of action. (See Barber v. Chang (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 1456, 1463 [duty of landlords to keep premises reasonably safe for tenants]; see also Martinez v. Bank of America Nat. Trust & Sav. Ass'n (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 883, 890-891 [liability when landlord has actual knowledge of the hazard].) The complaint properly alleges the theory of negligence per se and that defendant violated California Code of Regulations, Title 8, section 3231. The negligence per se doctrine creates a presumption of negligence that affects the burden of proof. (Evid. Code, Sec. 669 subd. (a)(1)-(3); see Quiroz v. Seventh Ave. Center (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1285 [negligence arises from the violation of a statute which was enacted to protect a class of persons of which the plaintiff is a member against the type of harm that the plaintiff suffered as a result of the violation].)

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that is also in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2021 Continued To May-04-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-09-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KIM LAVALLE's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is Off calendar for failure to comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41. Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41 requires a party, before filing the demurrer, to "meet and confer in person or by telephone" with the party who filed the complaint and to file a declaration stating the means by which the parties met and conferred. The January 5, 2021 "Declaration re: Meet and Confer Obligation" is insufficient. Defendant shall immediately meet and confer with the plaintiff. Defendant's response to the complaint is due no later than March 9, 2021.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
